expected_output: Type 2 diabetes is a condition where the body becomes resistant to insulin or produces insufficient insulin over time. It is the most common type accounting for 90-95% of all cases.
context: Type 2 Diabetes: The most common type accounting for 90-95% of all diabetes cases. Occurs when the body becomes resistant to insulin or produces insufficient insulin over time. Develops gradually often over many years. Risk factors: obesity physical inactivity family history age (usually 45+). Can often be managed with lifestyle changes oral medications or insulin.
